      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "import pandas as pd\r\nfrom data_access.pd_dataframes import fetch_column\r\nfrom flask import session\r\nimport os\r\nimport json\r\nfrom typing import Optional\r\nfrom app.config.database_config import database_config\r\n\r\nclass DataManager:\r\n    _server_instances = []\r\n    _session_key = 'selected_database'\r\n\r\n    def __init__(self, source=None):\r\n        # Always load data from session, don't rely on singleton\r\n        self._load_data(source)\r\n\r\n    def _load_data(self, source=None):\r\n        # Prioritize explicit source parameter over session\r\n        if source and database_config.validate_source(source):\r\n            self._current_source = source\r\n        else:\r\n            # Try to get from session\r\n            session_source = self._get_session_source()\r\n            if session_source and database_config.validate_source(session_source):\r\n                self._current_source = session_source\r\n            else:\r\n                # Use default from config\r\n                self._current_source = database_config.get_default_source()\r\n        \r\n        # Save to session for consistency\r\n        self._save_session_source(self._current_source)",

        "originalContents": "import pandas as pd\r\nfrom data_access.pd_dataframes import fetch_column\r\nfrom flask import session\r\nimport os\r\nimport json\r\nfrom typing import Optional\r\nfrom app.config.database_config import database_config\r\n\r\nclass DataManager:\r\n    _server_instances = []\r\n    _session_key = 'selected_database'\r\n\r\n    def __init__(self, source=None):\r\n        # Always load data from session, don't rely on singleton\r\n        self._load_data(source)\r\n\r\n    def _load_data(self, source=None):\r\n        # Prioritize explicit source parameter over session\r\n        if source and database_config.validate_source(source):\r\n            self._current_source = source\r\n        else:\r\n            # Try to get from session\r\n            session_source = self._get_session_source()\r\n            if session_source and database_config.validate_source(session_source):\r\n                self._current_source = session_source\r\n            else:\r\n                # Use default from config\r\n                self._current_source = database_config.get_default_source()\r\n        \r\n        # Save to session for consistency\r\n        self._save_session_source(self._current_source)",

        "contents": "\"\"\"\r\nDatabase Configuration\r\nManages available data sources and their settings\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport os\r\nfrom typing import Dict, List, Optional\r\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\r\n\r\n@dataclass\r\nclass DataSourceConfig:\r\n    \"\"\"Configuration for a data source\"\"\"\r\n    filename: str\r\n    display_name: str\r\n    description: str\r\n    is_default: bool = False\r\n    is_enabled: bool = True\r\n    file_path: Optional[str] = None\r\n    \r\n    def __post_init__(self):\r\n        if self.file_path is None:\r\n            self.file_path = f'database/data/{self.filename}'",

        "originalContents": "\"\"\"\r\nDatabase Configuration\r\nManages available data sources and their settings\r\n\"\"\"\r\n\r\nimport os\r\nfrom typing import Dict, List, Optional\r\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\r\n\r\n@dataclass\r\nclass DataSourceConfig:\r\n    \"\"\"Configuration for a data source\"\"\"\r\n    filename: str\r\n    display_name: str\r\n    description: str\r\n    is_default: bool = False\r\n    is_enabled: bool = True\r\n    file_path: Optional[str] = None\r\n    \r\n    def __post_init__(self):\r\n        if self.file_path is None:\r\n            self.file_path = f'database/data/{self.filename}'",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "import pandas as pd\r\nfrom data_access.schema import Columns\r\n\r\ndef read(data_df: pd.DataFrame, columns: list[Columns] | Columns | None = None, filters: dict[Columns, list[str]]=None, unique: bool=False, as_list: bool=False):\r\n    \"\"\"Reads and filters data from a DataFrame, returning results in simple Python data types.\r\n\r\n    Args:\r\n        data_df (pd.DataFrame): Input DataFrame to read from.\r\n        columns (list[str] | str | None, optional): Column name(s) to extract. Can be a single string, list of strings,\r\n            or None to select all columns. Defaults to None.\r\n        filters (dict[str, list[str]], optional): Dictionary of filters to apply, where keys are column names\r\n            and values are lists of acceptable values. Defaults to None.\r\n        unique (bool, optional): If True, removes duplicate rows from the result. Defaults to False.\r\n        as_list (bool, optional): Controls output format. If True, returns Python lists/dicts. \r\n            If False, returns pandas DataFrame. Defaults to True.\r\n\r\n    Returns:\r\n        Union[list, list[dict], pd.DataFrame]: Depending on the parameters:\r\n            - If as_list=True and single column: returns list of values\r\n            - If as_list=True and multiple columns: returns list of dictionaries\r\n            - If as_list=False: returns pandas DataFrame\r\n\r\n    Examples:\r\n        \u003e\u003e\u003e df = pd.DataFrame({'A': [1, 2, 2, 3], 'B': ['x', 'y', 'y', 'z']})\r\n        \u003e\u003e\u003e read(df, 'A')\r\n        [1, 2, 2, 3]\r\n        \r\n        \u003e\u003e\u003e read(df, 'A', unique=True)\r\n        [1, 2, 3]\r\n        \r\n        \u003e\u003e\u003e read(df, ['A', 'B'], filters={'A': [2, 3]})\r\n        [{'A': 2, 'B': 'y'}, {'A': 2, 'B': 'y'}, {'A': 3, 'B': 'z'}]\r\n        \r\n        \u003e\u003e\u003e read(df, ['A', 'B'], as_list=False)\r\n           A  B\r\n        0  1  x\r\n        1  2  y\r\n        2  2  y\r\n        3  3  z\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    if filters is None:\r\n        filters = {}",
        "detailedLines": [

          },
          {

          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "            If False, returns pandas DataFrame. Defaults to True.\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "\r"
          },

            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "    Returns:\r"

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "            - If as_list=True and multiple columns: returns list of dictionaries\r"
          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "        \u003e\u003e\u003e df = pd.DataFrame({'A': [1, 2, 2, 3], 'B': ['x', 'y', 'y', 'z']})\r"

            "text": "        3  3  z\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "    \"\"\"\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "    if filters is None:\r"

        "originalContents": "import pandas as pd\r\nfrom data_access.schema import Columns\r\n\r\ndef read(data_df: pd.DataFrame, columns: list[Columns] | Columns | None = None, filters: dict[Columns, list[str]]=None, unique: bool=False, as_list: bool=False):\r\n    \"\"\"Reads and filters data from a DataFrame, returning results in simple Python data types.\r\n\r\n    Args:\r\n        data_df (pd.DataFrame): Input DataFrame to read from.\r\n        columns (list[str] | str | None, optional): Column name(s) to extract. Can be a single string, list of strings,\r\n            or None to select all columns. Defaults to None.\r\n        filters (dict[str, list[str]], optional): Dictionary of filters to apply, where keys are column names\r\n            and values are lists of acceptable values. Defaults to None.\r\n        unique (bool, optional): If True, removes duplicate rows from the result. Defaults to False.\r\n        as_list (bool, optional): Controls output format. If True, returns Python lists/dicts. \r\n            If False, returns pandas DataFrame. Defaults to True.\r\n\r\n    Returns:\r\n        Union[list, list[dict], pd.DataFrame]: Depending on the parameters:\r\n            - If as_list=True and single column: returns list of values\r\n            - If as_list=True and multiple columns: returns list of dictionaries\r\n            - If as_list=False: returns pandas DataFrame\r\n\r\n    Examples:\r\n        \u003e\u003e\u003e df = pd.DataFrame({'A': [1, 2, 2, 3], 'B': ['x', 'y', 'y', 'z']})\r\n        \u003e\u003e\u003e read(df, 'A')\r\n        [1, 2, 2, 3]\r\n        \r\n        \u003e\u003e\u003e read(df, 'A', unique=True)\r\n        [1, 2, 3]\r\n        \r\n        \u003e\u003e\u003e read(df, ['A', 'B'], filters={'A': [2, 3]})\r\n        [{'A': 2, 'B': 'y'}, {'A': 2, 'B': 'y'}, {'A': 3, 'B': 'z'}]\r\n        \r\n        \u003e\u003e\u003e read(df, ['A', 'B'], as_list=False)\r\n           A  B\r\n        0  1  x\r\n        1  2  y\r\n        2  2  y\r\n        3  3  z\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    if filters is None:\r\n        filters = {}",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "from data_access.adapters.data_adapter import DataAdapter\r\nfrom data_access.schema import Columns, ColumnsExtra\r\nimport pandas as pd\r\nimport pathlib\r\nimport operator\r\nfrom typing import List, Dict, Any, Optional, Tuple, Callable, Union\r\n\r\nfrom data_access.models.league_models import LeagueQuery\r\nfrom data_access.models.league_models import TeamSeasonPerformance, TeamWeeklyPerformance\r\nfrom data_access.models.raw_data_models import RawPlayerData, RawTeamData, RawLeagueData\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n# Define comparison operators\r\nOPERATORS = {\r\n    \"eq\": operator.eq,  # Equal\r\n    \"ne\": operator.ne,  # Not equal\r\n    \"lt\": operator.lt,  # Less than\r\n    \"le\": operator.le,  # Less than or equal\r\n    \"gt\": operator.gt,  # Greater than\r\n    \"ge\": operator.ge,  # Greater than or equal\r\n    \"in\": lambda x, y: x in y,  # In list\r\n    \"not_in\": lambda x, y: x not in y,  # Not in list\r\n    \"contains\": lambda x, y: y in x if isinstance(x, str) else False,  # String contains\r\n    \"startswith\": lambda x, y: x.startswith(y) if isinstance(x, str) else False,  # String starts with\r\n    \"endswith\": lambda x, y: x.endswith(y) if isinstance(x, str) else False,  # String ends with\r\n}",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "from data_access.adapters.data_adapter import DataAdapter\r"

            "lineNumber": 7,

          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "from data_access.models.league_models import LeagueQuery\r"

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "OPERATORS = {\r"

            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "    \"eq\": operator.eq,  # Equal\r"

          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "    \"ne\": operator.ne,  # Not equal\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "    \"lt\": operator.lt,  # Less than\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "    \"le\": operator.le,  # Less than or equal\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "    \"gt\": operator.gt,  # Greater than\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "    \"ge\": operator.ge,  # Greater than or equal\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "    \"in\": lambda x, y: x in y,  # In list\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "    \"not_in\": lambda x, y: x not in y,  # Not in list\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "    \"contains\": lambda x, y: y in x if isinstance(x, str) else False,  # String contains\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,

            "isSignature": true,

            "text": "class DataAdapterPandas(DataAdapter):\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "    def __init__(self, path_to_csv_data: pathlib.Path=None, df: pd.DataFrame=None, database: str=None):\r"

            "lineNumber": 31,

        ],
        "originalContents": "from data_access.adapters.data_adapter import DataAdapter\r\nfrom data_access.schema import Columns, ColumnsExtra\r\nimport pandas as pd\r\nimport pathlib\r\nimport operator\r\nfrom typing import List, Dict, Any, Optional, Tuple, Callable, Union\r\n\r\nfrom data_access.models.league_models import LeagueQuery\r\nfrom data_access.models.league_models import TeamSeasonPerformance, TeamWeeklyPerformance\r\nfrom data_access.models.raw_data_models import RawPlayerData, RawTeamData, RawLeagueData\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n# Define comparison operators\r\nOPERATORS = {\r\n    \"eq\": operator.eq,  # Equal\r\n    \"ne\": operator.ne,  # Not equal\r\n    \"lt\": operator.lt,  # Less than\r\n    \"le\": operator.le,  # Less than or equal\r\n    \"gt\": operator.gt,  # Greater than\r\n    \"ge\": operator.ge,  # Greater than or equal\r\n    \"in\": lambda x, y: x in y,  # In list\r\n    \"not_in\": lambda x, y: x not in y,  # Not in list\r\n    \"contains\": lambda x, y: y in x if isinstance(x, str) else False,  # String contains\r\n    \"startswith\": lambda x, y: x.startswith(y) if isinstance(x, str) else False,  # String starts with\r\n    \"endswith\": lambda x, y: x.endswith(y) if isinstance(x, str) else False,  # String ends with\r\n}",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "# Database Switching System Guide\r\n\r\n## Overview\r\n\r\nThe new database switching system provides a reliable, session-based approach to switching between different data sources in the League Analyzer application. This system addresses the instability issues that occurred in production environments.\r\n\r\n## Key Features\r\n\r\n### 1. **Session-Based State Management**\r\n- Database selection is persisted in Flask sessions\r\n- Survives server restarts and worker process changes\r\n- User-specific database preferences\r\n\r\n### 2. **Robust Error Handling**\r\n- Automatic rollback on failed database switches\r\n- Graceful fallback to default data source\r\n- Comprehensive error logging and user notifications\r\n\r\n### 3. **Race Condition Prevention**\r\n- Prevents multiple simultaneous database switch requests\r\n- Loading states and UI feedback\r\n- Request deduplication\r\n\r\n### 4. **Centralized Configuration**\r\n- All data sources configured in one place\r\n- Easy to add new data sources\r\n- Validation of data source availability\r\n\r\n## Architecture\r\n\r\n### Components\r\n\r\n#### 1. **DatabaseConfig** (&#96;app/config/database_config.py&#96;)\r\n- Centralized configuration management\r\n- Data source validation and metadata\r\n- Default source management\r\n\r\n#### 2. **DataManager** (&#96;app/services/data_manager.py&#96;)\r\n- Session-based state persistence\r\n- Error handling and rollback logic\r\n- Server instance refresh coordination\r\n\r\n#### 3. **DatabaseSelector** (&#96;app/templates/components/database_selector.html&#96;)\r\n- Modern UI component with loading states\r\n- Real-time feedback and notifications\r\n- Event-driven architecture\r\n\r\n#### 4. **API Routes** (&#96;app/routes/main.py&#96;)\r\n- RESTful endpoints for database operations\r\n- Comprehensive error responses\r\n- Detailed source information\r\n\r\n## Usage\r\n\r\n### For Users\r\n\r\n1. **Switching Databases**\r\n   - Click the database selector in the navbar\r\n   - Choose from available data sources\r\n   - Wait for confirmation of successful switch\r\n\r\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "# Database Switching System Guide\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "## Overview\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "The new database switching system provides a reliable, session-based approach to switching between different data sources in the League Analyzer application. This system addresses the instability issues that occurred in production environments.\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "## Key Features\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "### 1. **Session-Based State Management**\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "- Database selection is persisted in Flask sessions\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "- Survives server restarts and worker process changes\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "- User-specific database preferences\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "### 2. **Robust Error Handling**\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "- Automatic rollback on failed database switches\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "- Graceful fallback to default data source\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,

          {
            "lineNumber": 53,

          {
            "lineNumber": 55,
            "text": "### For Users\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57,
            "text": "1. **Switching Databases**\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58,
            "text": "   - Click the database selector in the navbar\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59,
            "text": "   - Choose from available data sources\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60,
            "text": "   - Wait for confirmation of successful switch\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62,
            "text": "2. **Visual Feedback**\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 63,
            "text": "   - Loading spinner during switch\r"
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "# Database Switching System Guide\r\n\r\n## Overview\r\n\r\nThe new database switching system provides a reliable, session-based approach to switching between different data sources in the League Analyzer application. This system addresses the instability issues that occurred in production environments.\r\n\r\n## Key Features\r\n\r\n### 1. **Session-Based State Management**\r\n- Database selection is persisted in Flask sessions\r\n- Survives server restarts and worker process changes\r\n- User-specific database preferences\r\n\r\n### 2. **Robust Error Handling**\r\n- Automatic rollback on failed database switches\r\n- Graceful fallback to default data source\r\n- Comprehensive error logging and user notifications\r\n\r\n### 3. **Race Condition Prevention**\r\n- Prevents multiple simultaneous database switch requests\r\n- Loading states and UI feedback\r\n- Request deduplication\r\n\r\n### 4. **Centralized Configuration**\r\n- All data sources configured in one place\r\n- Easy to add new data sources\r\n- Validation of data source availability\r\n\r\n## Architecture\r\n\r\n### Components\r\n\r\n#### 1. **DatabaseConfig** (&#96;app/config/database_config.py&#96;)\r\n- Centralized configuration management\r\n- Data source validation and metadata\r\n- Default source management\r\n\r\n#### 2. **DataManager** (&#96;app/services/data_manager.py&#96;)\r\n- Session-based state persistence\r\n- Error handling and rollback logic\r\n- Server instance refresh coordination\r\n\r\n#### 3. **DatabaseSelector** (&#96;app/templates/components/database_selector.html&#96;)\r\n- Modern UI component with loading states\r\n- Real-time feedback and notifications\r\n- Event-driven architecture\r\n\r\n#### 4. **API Routes** (&#96;app/routes/main.py&#96;)\r\n- RESTful endpoints for database operations\r\n- Comprehensive error responses\r\n- Detailed source information\r\n\r\n## Usage\r\n\r\n### For Users\r\n\r\n1. **Switching Databases**\r\n   - Click the database selector in the navbar\r\n   - Choose from available data sources\r\n   - Wait for confirmation of successful switch\r\n\r\n",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 63
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "docs/DATABASE_SWITCHING_GUIDE.md"
      },
      "score": 0.2451172024011612
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "from flask import Blueprint, render_template, jsonify, request\r\nfrom app.services.league_service import LeagueService\r\nfrom data_access.schema import Columns, ColumnsExtra\r\nfrom app.services.i18n_service import i18n_service, Language\r\nfrom business_logic.statistics import query_database\r\nfrom data_access.adapters.data_adapter_factory import DataAdapterSelector\r\nimport traceback\r\nfrom app.services.data_dict import DataDict\r\n\r\nbp = Blueprint('league', __name__)\r\n\r\ndef get_league_service():\r\n    \"\"\"Helper function to get LeagueService with database parameter\"\"\"\r\n    database = request.args.get('database')\r\n    print(f\"🔄 get_league_service called with database: {database}\")\r\n    league_service = LeagueService(database=database)\r\n    print(f\"✅ LeagueService created with database: {getattr(league_service, 'database', 'None')}\")\r\n    return league_service",

        "originalContents": "from flask import Blueprint, render_template, jsonify, request\r\nfrom app.services.league_service import LeagueService\r\nfrom data_access.schema import Columns, ColumnsExtra\r\nfrom app.services.i18n_service import i18n_service, Language\r\nfrom business_logic.statistics import query_database\r\nfrom data_access.adapters.data_adapter_factory import DataAdapterSelector\r\nimport traceback\r\nfrom app.services.data_dict import DataDict\r\n\r\nbp = Blueprint('league', __name__)\r\n\r\ndef get_league_service():\r\n    \"\"\"Helper function to get LeagueService with database parameter\"\"\"\r\n    database = request.args.get('database')\r\n    print(f\"🔄 get_league_service called with database: {database}\")\r\n    league_service = LeagueService(database=database)\r\n    print(f\"✅ LeagueService created with database: {getattr(league_service, 'database', 'None')}\")\r\n    return league_service",

{
  "codeResults": [
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {\r\n    console.log('🔄 Loading latest events with database:', getCurrentDatabase());\r\n    \r\n    // Use fetchWithDatabase to include the database parameter\r\n    fetchWithDatabase('{{ url_for(\"league.get_latest_events\") }}')\r\n        .then(response =\u003e response.json())\r\n        .then(data =\u003e {\r\n            console.log('✅ Latest events loaded:', data);\r\n            const eventsList = document.getElementById('latestEvents');\r\n            if (data \u0026\u0026 Array.isArray(data)) {\r\n                eventsList.innerHTML = data.map(event =\u003e &#96;\r\n                    \u003cli class=\"list-group-item\"\u003e\r\n                        \u003ca href=\"{{ url_for('league.stats') }}?season=${encodeURIComponent(event.Season)}\u0026league=${encodeURIComponent(event.League)}\u0026week=${event.Week}\u0026database=${encodeURIComponent(getCurrentDatabase())}\" \r\n                           class=\"text-decoration-none\"\u003e\r\n                            ${event.Season} - ${event.League} - Woche ${event.Week}\r\n                            \u003csmall class=\"text-muted\"\u003e(${event.Date})\u003c/small\u003e\r\n                        \u003c/a\u003e\r\n                    \u003c/li\u003e\r\n                &#96;).join('');\r\n            } else {\r\n                eventsList.innerHTML = '\u003cli class=\"list-group-item\"\u003eKeine Events gefunden\u003c/li\u003e';\r\n            }\r\n        })\r\n        .catch(error =\u003e {\r\n            console.error('❌ Error fetching events:', error);\r\n            const eventsList = document.getElementById('latestEvents');\r\n            eventsList.innerHTML = '\u003cli class=\"list-group-item text-danger\"\u003eFehler beim Laden der Events\u003c/li\u003e';\r\n        });\r\n});\r\n\u003c/script\u003e\r\n{% endblock %} ",

            "lineNumber": 65,
            "text": "        })\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66,
            "text": "        .catch(error =\u003e {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 67,
            "text": "            console.error('❌ Error fetching events:', error);\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 68,
            "text": "            const eventsList = document.getElementById('latestEvents');\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 69,
            "text": "            eventsList.innerHTML = '\u003cli class=\"list-group-item text-danger\"\u003eFehler beim Laden der Events\u003c/li\u003e';\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 70,
            "text": "        });\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 71,
            "text": "});\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 72,
            "text": "\u003c/script\u003e\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 73,
            "text": "{% endblock %} "
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {\r\n    console.log('🔄 Loading latest events with database:', getCurrentDatabase());\r\n    \r\n    // Use fetchWithDatabase to include the database parameter\r\n    fetchWithDatabase('{{ url_for(\"league.get_latest_events\") }}')\r\n        .then(response =\u003e response.json())\r\n        .then(data =\u003e {\r\n            console.log('✅ Latest events loaded:', data);\r\n            const eventsList = document.getElementById('latestEvents');\r\n            if (data \u0026\u0026 Array.isArray(data)) {\r\n                eventsList.innerHTML = data.map(event =\u003e &#96;\r\n                    \u003cli class=\"list-group-item\"\u003e\r\n                        \u003ca href=\"{{ url_for('league.stats') }}?season=${encodeURIComponent(event.Season)}\u0026league=${encodeURIComponent(event.League)}\u0026week=${event.Week}\u0026database=${encodeURIComponent(getCurrentDatabase())}\" \r\n                           class=\"text-decoration-none\"\u003e\r\n                            ${event.Season} - ${event.League} - Woche ${event.Week}\r\n                            \u003csmall class=\"text-muted\"\u003e(${event.Date})\u003c/small\u003e\r\n                        \u003c/a\u003e\r\n                    \u003c/li\u003e\r\n                &#96;).join('');\r\n            } else {\r\n                eventsList.innerHTML = '\u003cli class=\"list-group-item\"\u003eKeine Events gefunden\u003c/li\u003e';\r\n            }\r\n        })\r\n        .catch(error =\u003e {\r\n            console.error('❌ Error fetching events:', error);\r\n            const eventsList = document.getElementById('latestEvents');\r\n            eventsList.innerHTML = '\u003cli class=\"list-group-item text-danger\"\u003eFehler beim Laden der Events\u003c/li\u003e';\r\n        });\r\n});\r\n\u003c/script\u003e\r\n{% endblock %} ",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 15,
            "line": 72
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 41
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "app/templates/index.html"
      },
      "score": 0.4326171875
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/get_latest_events')\r\ndef get_latest_events():\r\n    try:\r\n        # Get limit parameter with default value of 5\r\n        limit = request.args.get('limit', default=10, type=int)\r\n        \r\n        print(f\"Latest Events - Received request with limit={limit}\")\r\n        \r\n        # Validate limit\r\n        if limit \u003c= 0:\r\n            return jsonify({'error': 'Limit must be greater than 0'}), 400\r\n            \r\n        league_service = get_league_service()\r\n        events = league_service.get_latest_events(limit=limit)\r\n        #print(\"league_routes.get_latest_events\")\r\n        #print(events)\r\n        print(f\"Found {len(events)} latest events\")\r\n        return jsonify(events)\r\n        \r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"Error in get_latest_events: {str(e)}\")\r\n        print(traceback.format_exc())\r\n        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 500",

        ],
        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/get_latest_events')\r\ndef get_latest_events():\r\n    try:\r\n        # Get limit parameter with default value of 5\r\n        limit = request.args.get('limit', default=10, type=int)\r\n        \r\n        print(f\"Latest Events - Received request with limit={limit}\")\r\n        \r\n        # Validate limit\r\n        if limit \u003c= 0:\r\n            return jsonify({'error': 'Limit must be greater than 0'}), 400\r\n            \r\n        league_service = get_league_service()\r\n        events = league_service.get_latest_events(limit=limit)\r\n        #print(\"league_routes.get_latest_events\")\r\n        #print(events)\r\n        print(f\"Found {len(events)} latest events\")\r\n        return jsonify(events)\r\n        \r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"Error in get_latest_events: {str(e)}\")\r\n        print(traceback.format_exc())\r\n        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 500",

        "contents": "class Server:\r\n    # fetches basic dataframes from data adapter\r\n    # converts basic dataframes to aggregated dataframes\r\n    # forwards aggregated dataframes to app\n    ...\n    def get_special_matches(self, team_name: str=None, amount: int=3):\n\r\n\r\n        ...\n\r\n\r\n        # highest scores\r\n        highest_scores = data_team.sort_values(by=Columns.score, ascending=False).head(amount)\r\n\r\n        # lowest scores\r\n        lowest_scores = data_team.sort_values(by=Columns.score, ascending=True).head(amount)\r\n\r\n        # biggest win margin\r\n        biggest_win_margin = data_team.sort_values(by=ColumnsExtra.margin, ascending=False).head(amount)\r\n\r\n        # biggest loss margin\r\n        biggest_loss_margin = data_team.sort_values(by=ColumnsExtra.margin, ascending=True).head(amount)\r\n\r\n        # highest scoring match ( own score + opponent score )\r\n        #highest_scoring_match = data_team.sort_values(by=2 * Columns.score - ColumnsExtra.margin, ascending=False).head(amount)\r\n\r\n        # lowest scoring match ( own score + opponent score )\r\n        #lowest_scoring_match = data_team.sort_values(by=2 * Columns.score - ColumnsExtra.margin, ascending=True).head(amount)\r\n\r\n        # compose dictionary\r\n        special_matches = {\r\n            'highest_scores': highest_scores.to_dict(orient='records'),\r\n            'lowest_scores': lowest_scores.to_dict(orient='records'),\r\n            'biggest_win_margin': biggest_win_margin.to_dict(orient='records'),\r\n            'biggest_loss_margin': biggest_loss_margin.to_dict(orient='records')\r\n            #'highest_scoring_match': highest_scoring_match.to_dict(orient='records'),\r\n            #'lowest_scoring_match': lowest_scoring_match.to_dict(orient='records'),\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        \"\"\"Returns the special matches for a given team\"\"\"\r\n        return special_matches\r\n    \r\n    def get_latest_events(self, limit=5) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n        \"\"\"Get the latest league events based on date.\"\"\"\r\n        print(\"server.get_latest_events\")\r\n\r\n        # Get unique combinations of season, league, week, and date\r\n        data =  self.data_adapter.get_latest_events(limit)\r\n        print(data)\r\n        return data",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "{% extends \"base.html\" %}\r\n\r\n{% block title %}Home{% endblock %}\r\n\r\n{% block content %}\r\n\u003c!--h1\u003eBowling Dashboard\u003c/h1--\u003e\r\n\u003cdiv class=\"row mt-4\"\u003e\r\n    \u003cdiv class=\"col-md-6\"\u003e\r\n        \u003cdiv class=\"card mb-4\"\u003e\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"card-header\"\u003e\r\n                Info\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"card-body\"\u003e\r\n                \u003c!-- We'll add stats here later --\u003e\r\n                \u003cp\u003eDiese Seite ist ein Proof of Concept zur Darstellung von Ergebnissen und Statistiken rund um Bowling Ligen, Tuniere und Pokalwettbewerbe.\u003c/p\u003e\r\n                \u003cp\u003eDie Auswertungen sind oben über die Navigation zu \"League Stats\" und \"Player Stats\" zu finden.\u003c/p\u003e\r\n                \u003cp\u003eHauptaugenmerk liegt derzeit auf den Ligastatistiken. Alle Daten sind fiktiv, jedoch ist die Seite so aufgabeut, dass reale Daten einfach importiert werden könnten.\u003c/p\u003e\r\n                \u003cp\u003eBitte kommt mit Feedback und Vorschlägen gerne per Whatsapp oder im Traning auf mich zu.\u003c/p\u003e\r\n                \u003cp\u003e  Cheers, Chris\u003c/p\u003e\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n        \u003c/div\u003e\r\n\r\n\r\n    \u003c/div\u003e\r\n\r\n    \r\n    \u003cdiv class=\"col-md-6\"\u003e\r\n        \u003cdiv class=\"card\"\u003e\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"card-header\"\u003e\r\n                Letzte Events\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"card-body\"\u003e\r\n                \u003cul class=\"list-group\" id=\"latestEvents\"\u003e\r\n                    \u003c!-- Will be populated via JavaScript --\u003e\r\n                \u003c/ul\u003e\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n        \u003c/div\u003e\r\n    \u003c/div\u003e\r\n\u003c/div\u003e\r\n\r\n\u003c!-- Add JavaScript for latest events --\u003e\r\n\u003cscript\u003e\r\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "{% extends \"base.html\" %}\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "{% block title %}Home{% endblock %}\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "{% block content %}\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "\u003c!--h1\u003eBowling Dashboard\u003c/h1--\u003e\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "\u003cdiv class=\"row mt-4\"\u003e\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "    \u003cdiv class=\"col-md-6\"\u003e\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "        \u003cdiv class=\"card mb-4\"\u003e\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "            \u003cdiv class=\"card-header\"\u003e\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "                Info\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "            \u003c/div\u003e\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "            \u003cdiv class=\"card-body\"\u003e\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "                \u003c!-- We'll add stats here later --\u003e\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "                \u003cp\u003eDiese Seite ist ein Proof of Concept zur Darstellung von Ergebnissen und Statistiken rund um Bowling Ligen, Tuniere und Pokalwettbewerbe.\u003c/p\u003e\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "                \u003cp\u003eDie Auswertungen sind oben über die Navigation zu \"League Stats\" und \"Player Stats\" zu finden.\u003c/p\u003e\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "                \u003cp\u003eHauptaugenmerk liegt derzeit auf den Ligastatistiken. Alle Daten sind fiktiv, jedoch ist die Seite so aufgabeut, dass reale Daten einfach importiert werden könnten.\u003c/p\u003e\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "                \u003cp\u003eBitte kommt mit Feedback und Vorschlägen gerne per Whatsapp oder im Traning auf mich zu.\u003c/p\u003e\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "                \u003cp\u003e  Cheers, Chris\u003c/p\u003e\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "            \u003c/div\u003e\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "        \u003c/div\u003e\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "    \u003c/div\u003e\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "    \r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "    \u003cdiv class=\"col-md-6\"\u003e\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "        \u003cdiv class=\"card\"\u003e\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "            \u003cdiv class=\"card-header\"\u003e\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "                Letzte Events\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "            \u003c/div\u003e\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "            \u003cdiv class=\"card-body\"\u003e\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "                    \u003c!-- Will be populated via JavaScript --\u003e\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "                \u003c/ul\u003e\r"

          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "            \u003c/div\u003e\r"

          {
            "lineNumber": 37,

            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "\u003c!-- Add JavaScript for latest events --\u003e\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "\u003cscript\u003e\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "    "
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "{% extends \"base.html\" %}\r\n\r\n{% block title %}Home{% endblock %}\r\n\r\n{% block content %}\r\n\u003c!--h1\u003eBowling Dashboard\u003c/h1--\u003e\r\n\u003cdiv class=\"row mt-4\"\u003e\r\n    \u003cdiv class=\"col-md-6\"\u003e\r\n        \u003cdiv class=\"card mb-4\"\u003e\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"card-header\"\u003e\r\n                Info\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"card-body\"\u003e\r\n                \u003c!-- We'll add stats here later --\u003e\r\n                \u003cp\u003eDiese Seite ist ein Proof of Concept zur Darstellung von Ergebnissen und Statistiken rund um Bowling Ligen, Tuniere und Pokalwettbewerbe.\u003c/p\u003e\r\n                \u003cp\u003eDie Auswertungen sind oben über die Navigation zu \"League Stats\" und \"Player Stats\" zu finden.\u003c/p\u003e\r\n                \u003cp\u003eHauptaugenmerk liegt derzeit auf den Ligastatistiken. Alle Daten sind fiktiv, jedoch ist die Seite so aufgabeut, dass reale Daten einfach importiert werden könnten.\u003c/p\u003e\r\n                \u003cp\u003eBitte kommt mit Feedback und Vorschlägen gerne per Whatsapp oder im Traning auf mich zu.\u003c/p\u003e\r\n                \u003cp\u003e  Cheers, Chris\u003c/p\u003e\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n        \u003c/div\u003e\r\n\r\n\r\n    \u003c/div\u003e\r\n\r\n    \r\n    \u003cdiv class=\"col-md-6\"\u003e\r\n        \u003cdiv class=\"card\"\u003e\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"card-header\"\u003e\r\n                Letzte Events\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n            \u003cdiv class=\"card-body\"\u003e\r\n                \u003cul class=\"list-group\" id=\"latestEvents\"\u003e\r\n                    \u003c!-- Will be populated via JavaScript --\u003e\r\n                \u003c/ul\u003e\r\n            \u003c/div\u003e\r\n        \u003c/div\u003e\r\n    \u003c/div\u003e\r\n\u003c/div\u003e\r\n\r\n\u003c!-- Add JavaScript for latest events --\u003e\r\n\u003cscript\u003e\r\n",

            "column": 4,
            "line": 43
          },

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n    ...\n    def get_league_performance_chart(self, season: str, league: str, team_id: Optional[str] = None) -\u003e PlotData:\n\r\n        \r\n        ...\n\r\n        \r\n        # Weeks completed tile\r\n        if standings.teams and standings.teams[0].weekly_performances:\r\n            completed_weeks = len(set(p.week for p in standings.teams[0].weekly_performances))\r\n            tiles.append(\r\n                TileData(\r\n                    title=\"Weeks Completed\",\r\n                    value=str(completed_weeks),\r\n                    subtitle=f\"of {completed_weeks + 2}\",  # Assuming 2 more weeks to go\r\n                    type=\"progress\",\r\n                    chart_data=[completed_weeks, completed_weeks + 2]\r\n                )\r\n            )\r\n        \r\n        return tiles\r\n    \r\n    def get_latest_events(self, limit: int = 5) -\u003e List[Dict[str, Any]]:\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        Get the latest league events.\r\n        \r\n        Args:\r\n            limit: Maximum number of events to return\r\n            \r\n        Returns:\r\n            List of event dictionaries\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        # Get all events from the adapter\r\n        events_df = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(\r\n            filters={},\r\n            sort_by=\"Date\",\r\n            ascending=False,\r\n            limit=limit\r\n        )\r\n        \r\n        # If no events, return empty list\r\n        if events_df.empty:\r\n            return []\r\n        \r\n        # Convert to list of dictionaries\r\n        events = []\r\n        for _, row in events_df.iterrows():\r\n            event = {}\r\n            for col in [\"Season\", \"League\", \"Week\", \"Date\"]:\r\n                if col in row:\r\n                    event[col] = row[col]\r\n            events.append(event)\r\n        \r\n        return events\r\n\r\n    def get_weeks(self, league_name: str = None, season: str = None) -\u003e List[int]:\r\n        \"\"\"Get available weeks for a league and season\"\"\"\r\n        #if not league_name or not season:\r\n            # Return empty list if parameters are missing\r\n        #    return []\r\n        return self.adapter.get_weeks(season, league_name)",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n        try {\r\n            console.log(&#96;🔄 Switching database to: ${sourceId}&#96;);\r\n            \r\n            // First, try to switch via AJAX to avoid page reload\r\n            const response = await fetch('/main/switch-database', {\r\n                method: 'POST',\r\n                headers: {\r\n                    'Content-Type': 'application/json',\r\n                },\r\n                body: JSON.stringify({ database: sourceId })\r\n            });\r\n            \r\n            const data = await response.json();\r\n            \r\n            if (data.success) {\r\n                console.log('✅ Database switched successfully via AJAX');\r\n                \r\n                // Update URL without page reload\r\n                const url = new URL(window.location);\r\n                url.searchParams.set('database', sourceId);\r\n                window.history.pushState({}, '', url.toString());\r\n                \r\n                // Update the display\r\n                this.currentSource = sourceId;\r\n                this.updateDisplay();\r\n                this.renderOptions();\r\n                \r\n                // Trigger a global event to notify other components\r\n                window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('databaseChanged', { \r\n                    detail: { database: sourceId } \r\n                }));\r\n                \r\n                // Show success message\r\n                this.showSuccess(&#96;Switched to ${this.sourcesInfo[sourceId]?.display_name || sourceId}&#96;);\r\n                \r\n            } else {\r\n                console.warn('AJAX switch failed, falling back to page reload');\r\n                // Fallback to page reload if AJAX fails\r\n                const url = new URL(window.location);\r\n                url.searchParams.set('database', sourceId);\r\n                window.location.href = url.toString();\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n        ",

        ],
        "originalContents": "\r\n        try {\r\n            console.log(&#96;🔄 Switching database to: ${sourceId}&#96;);\r\n            \r\n            // First, try to switch via AJAX to avoid page reload\r\n            const response = await fetch('/main/switch-database', {\r\n                method: 'POST',\r\n                headers: {\r\n                    'Content-Type': 'application/json',\r\n                },\r\n                body: JSON.stringify({ database: sourceId })\r\n            });\r\n            \r\n            const data = await response.json();\r\n            \r\n            if (data.success) {\r\n                console.log('✅ Database switched successfully via AJAX');\r\n                \r\n                // Update URL without page reload\r\n                const url = new URL(window.location);\r\n                url.searchParams.set('database', sourceId);\r\n                window.history.pushState({}, '', url.toString());\r\n                \r\n                // Update the display\r\n                this.currentSource = sourceId;\r\n                this.updateDisplay();\r\n                this.renderOptions();\r\n                \r\n                // Trigger a global event to notify other components\r\n                window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('databaseChanged', { \r\n                    detail: { database: sourceId } \r\n                }));\r\n                \r\n                // Show success message\r\n                this.showSuccess(&#96;Switched to ${this.sourcesInfo[sourceId]?.display_name || sourceId}&#96;);\r\n                \r\n            } else {\r\n                console.warn('AJAX switch failed, falling back to page reload');\r\n                // Fallback to page reload if AJAX fails\r\n                const url = new URL(window.location);\r\n                url.searchParams.set('database', sourceId);\r\n                window.location.href = url.toString();\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n        ",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 12,
            "line": 327
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 281
          }
        },

      },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class DataAdapterPandas(DataAdapter):\n    ...\n    def get_filtered_data__deprecated(self, columns: List[Columns]=None, filters_eq: dict=None, filters_lt: dict=None, filters_gt: dict=None, print_debug: bool=False) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\n\r\n        \r\n        ...\n\r\n        \r\n        try:\r\n            # Get unique weeks, sort them, and convert to list\r\n            weeks = sorted(filtered_df[Columns.week].unique().tolist())\r\n            # Filter out None/NaN values if any\r\n            weeks = [week for week in weeks if week is not None]\r\n            #print(f\"Available weeks: {weeks}\")  # Debug output\r\n            return weeks\r\n        except Exception as e:\r\n            print(f\"Error getting weeks: {str(e)}\")\r\n            return []\r\n    \r\n    def get_league_match_day(self, league_name: str, season: str, week: int) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n        return self.df[(self.df[Columns.league_name] == league_name) \u0026 (self.df[Columns.season] == season) \u0026 (self.df[Columns.week] == week)]\r\n    \r\n    def get_league_season(self, league_name: str, season: str, week: int=None) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n        if week is None:\r\n            return self.df[self.df[Columns.league_name] == league_name][self.df[Columns.season] == season]\r\n        else:\r\n            return self.df[self.df[Columns.league_name] == league_name][self.df[Columns.season] == season][self.df[Columns.week] \u003c= week]\r\n\r\n    def get_latest_events(self, limit=5):\r\n        \"\"\"Get the latest league events based on date.\"\"\"\r\n        # Get unique combinations of season, league, week, and date\r\n        events = self.df[[Columns.season, Columns.league_name, Columns.week, Columns.date]].drop_duplicates()\r\n\r\n        # Sort by date in descending order and get the latest events\r\n        latest_events = events.sort_values(by=Columns.date, ascending=False).head(limit)\r\n\r\n        return latest_events",

        "contents": "                \u003cscript\u003e\r\n                    // Preserve database parameter in navigation links\r\n                    document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {\r\n                        const currentDatabase = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search).get('database');\r\n                        \r\n                        if (currentDatabase) {\r\n                            // Update all navigation links to include the database parameter\r\n                            const navLinks = [\r\n                                'playerStatsLink',\r\n                                'leagueStatsLink', \r\n                                'teamStatsLink',\r\n                                'apiTestLink',\r\n                                'impressumLink'\r\n                            ];\r\n                            \r\n                            navLinks.forEach(linkId =\u003e {\r\n                                const link = document.getElementById(linkId);\r\n                                if (link) {\r\n                                    const url = new URL(link.href);\r\n                                    url.searchParams.set('database', currentDatabase);\r\n                                    link.href = url.toString();\r\n                                }\r\n                            });\r\n                        }\r\n                    });\r\n                    \r\n                    // Palette dropdown handler\r\n",

        "contents": "} catch (error) {\r\n            console.error('Error switching database:', error);\r\n            console.log('Falling back to page reload method');\r\n            \r\n            // Fallback to page reload\r\n            const url = new URL(window.location);\r\n            url.searchParams.set('database', sourceId);\r\n            window.location.href = url.toString();\r\n        } finally {\r\n            this.isLoading = false;\r\n            this.hideLoadingState();\r\n        }\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    async refreshDynamicComponents() {\r\n        console.log('🔄 Refreshing dynamic components after database switch...');\r\n        \r\n        // Wait a moment for the server to fully process the database switch\r\n        await new Promise(resolve =\u003e setTimeout(resolve, 500));\r\n        \r\n        // Trigger various component refresh methods\r\n        const refreshMethods = [\r\n            // League components\r\n            'updateSeasonButtons',\r\n            'updateLeagueButtons', \r\n            'updateWeekButtons',\r\n            'updateTeamButtons',\r\n            'updateTableLeagueHistory',\r\n             "text": "            'renderContent'\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 380,
            "text": "        ];\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 381,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 382,
            "text": "        // Try to call available refresh methods\r"
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "} catch (error) {\r\n            console.error('Error switching database:', error);\r\n            console.log('Falling back to page reload method');\r\n            \r\n            // Fallback to page reload\r\n            const url = new URL(window.location);\r\n            url.searchParams.set('database', sourceId);\r\n            window.location.href = url.toString();\r\n        } finally {\r\n            this.isLoading = false;\r\n            this.hideLoadingState();\r\n        }\r\n    }\r\n\r\n    async refreshDynamicComponents() {\r\n        console.log('🔄 Refreshing dynamic components after database switch...');\r\n        \r\n        // Wait a moment for the server to fully process the database switch\r\n        await new Promise(resolve =\u003e setTimeout(resolve, 500));\r\n        \r\n        // Trigger various component refresh methods\r\n        const refreshMethods = [\r\n            // League components\r\n            'updateSeasonButtons',\r\n            'updateLeagueButtons', \r\n            'updateWeekButtons',\r\n            'updateTeamButtons',\r\n            'updateTableLeagueHistory',\r\n            'updateTableLeagueWeek',\r\n            'updateTableTeamDetails',\r\n            'updateChartsLeague',\r\n            \r\n            // Team components\r\n            'updateTeamSelect',\r\n            'updateSeasonButtons',\r\n            'updateWeeksData',\r\n            'updateTeamHistory',\r\n            'updateLeagueComparison',\r\n            'updateClutchAnalysis',\r\n            'updateConsistencyMetrics',\r\n            'updateSpecialMatches',\r\n            \r\n            // Player components\r\n            'loadPlayerStats',\r\n            'displayLifetimeStats',\r\n            'displaySeasonStats',\r\n            'updateTrendChart',\r\n            \r\n            // General refresh methods\r\n            'refreshCurrentData',\r\n            'handleDataSourceChange',\r\n            'updateContent',\r\n            'renderContent'\r\n        ];\r\n",
        "range": {

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class URLStateManager {\n\r\n    \r\n    ...\n\r\n    \r\n    /**\r\n     * Update URL with current state\r\n     */\r\n    updateUrl(newState, replaceHistory = false) {\r\n        // Merge new state with existing state\r\n        this.state = { ...this.state, ...newState };\r\n        \r\n        // Build URL parameters\r\n        const params = new URLSearchParams();\r\n        Object.entries(this.state).forEach(([key, value]) =\u003e {\r\n            if (value \u0026\u0026 value !== '' \u0026\u0026 value !== 'main') {\r\n                params.set(key, value);\r\n            }\r\n        });\r\n        \r\n        // Update browser URL\r\n        const newUrl = &#96;${window.location.pathname}?${params.toString()}&#96;;\r\n        \r\n        if (replaceHistory) {\r\n            window.history.replaceState(this.state, '', newUrl);\r\n        } else {\r\n            window.history.pushState(this.state, '', newUrl);\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        console.log('URL updated:', newUrl, 'State:', this.state);\r\n    }",

        "originalContents": "\r\n    \r\n    /**\r\n     * Update URL with current state\r\n     */\r\n    updateUrl(newState, replaceHistory = false) {\r\n        // Merge new state with existing state\r\n        this.state = { ...this.state, ...newState };\r\n        \r\n        // Build URL parameters\r\n        const params = new URLSearchParams();\r\n        Object.entries(this.state).forEach(([key, value]) =\u003e {\r\n            if (value \u0026\u0026 value !== '' \u0026\u0026 value !== 'main') {\r\n                params.set(key, value);\r\n            }\r\n        });\r\n        \r\n        // Update browser URL\r\n        const newUrl = &#96;${window.location.pathname}?${params.toString()}&#96;;\r\n        \r\n        if (replaceHistory) {\r\n            window.history.replaceState(this.state, '', newUrl);\r\n        } else {\r\n            window.history.pushState(this.state, '', newUrl);\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        console.log('URL updated:', newUrl, 'State:', this.state);\r\n    }",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n        // Try to call available refresh methods\r\n        for (const methodName of refreshMethods) {\r\n            try {\r\n                if (typeof window[methodName] === 'function') {\r\n                    console.log(&#96;Calling ${methodName}...&#96;);\r\n                    await window[methodName]();\r\n                }\r\n            } catch (error) {\r\n                console.warn(&#96;Error calling ${methodName}:&#96;, error);\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        // Trigger specific events for different page types\r\n        const currentPath = window.location.pathname;\r\n        \r\n        if (currentPath.includes('/league/')) {\r\n            this.triggerLeaguePageRefresh();\r\n        } else if (currentPath.includes('/team/')) {\r\n            this.triggerTeamPageRefresh();\r\n        } else if (currentPath.includes('/player/')) {\r\n            this.triggerPlayerPageRefresh();\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        // Force a page refresh if no dynamic methods are available\r\n        setTimeout(() =\u003e {\r\n            if (!this.hasActiveComponents()) {\r\n                console.log('No active components detected, refreshing page...');\r\n                window.location.reload();\r\n            }\r\n        }, 2000);\r\n    }\r\n",

        "originalContents": "\r\n        // Try to call available refresh methods\r\n        for (const methodName of refreshMethods) {\r\n            try {\r\n                if (typeof window[methodName] === 'function') {\r\n                    console.log(&#96;Calling ${methodName}...&#96;);\r\n                    await window[methodName]();\r\n                }\r\n            } catch (error) {\r\n                console.warn(&#96;Error calling ${methodName}:&#96;, error);\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        // Trigger specific events for different page types\r\n        const currentPath = window.location.pathname;\r\n        \r\n        if (currentPath.includes('/league/')) {\r\n            this.triggerLeaguePageRefresh();\r\n        } else if (currentPath.includes('/team/')) {\r\n            this.triggerTeamPageRefresh();\r\n        } else if (currentPath.includes('/player/')) {\r\n            this.triggerPlayerPageRefresh();\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        // Force a page refresh if no dynamic methods are available\r\n        setTimeout(() =\u003e {\r\n            if (!this.hasActiveComponents()) {\r\n                console.log('No active components detected, refreshing page...');\r\n                window.location.reload();\r\n            }\r\n        }, 2000);\r\n    }\r\n",

        "originalContents": "\u003cnav class=\"navbar navbar-expand-lg navbar-dark mb-4\"\u003e\r\n    \u003cdiv class=\"container\"\u003e\r\n        \u003ca class=\"navbar-brand\" href=\"{{ url_for('main.index') }}\"\u003eBowl-A-Lyzer\u003c/a\u003e\r\n        \u003cbutton class=\"navbar-toggler\" type=\"button\" data-bs-toggle=\"collapse\" data-bs-target=\"#navbarNav\"\u003e\r\n            \u003cspan class=\"navbar-toggler-icon\"\u003e\u003c/span\u003e\r\n        \u003c/button\u003e\r\n        \u003cdiv class=\"collapse navbar-collapse\" id=\"navbarNav\"\u003e\r\n            \u003cul class=\"navbar-nav\"\u003e\r\n                \u003cli class=\"nav-item\"\u003e\r\n                    \u003ca class=\"nav-link\" href=\"{{ url_for('player.stats') }}\" id=\"playerStatsLink\"\u003ePlayer Stats\u003c/a\u003e\r\n                \u003c/li\u003e\r\n                \u003cli class=\"nav-item\"\u003e\r\n                    \u003ca class=\"nav-link\" href=\"{{ url_for('league.stats') }}\" id=\"leagueStatsLink\"\u003eLeague Stats\u003c/a\u003e\r\n                \u003c/li\u003e\r\n                \u003cli class=\"nav-item\"\u003e\r\n                    \u003ca class=\"nav-link\" href=\"{{ url_for('team.stats') }}\" id=\"teamStatsLink\"\u003eTeam Stats\u003c/a\u003e\r\n                \u003c/li\u003e\r\n                \u003cli class=\"nav-item\"\u003e\r\n                    \u003ca class=\"nav-link\" href=\"{{ url_for('main.test') }}\" id=\"apiTestLink\"\u003eAPI Test\u003c/a\u003e\r\n                \u003c/li\u003e\r\n            \u003c/ul\u003e\r\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "/**\r\n * URL State Manager\r\n * \r\n * Manages synchronization between application state and URL parameters\r\n * Provides browser history support and shareable links\r\n */\r\n\r\nclass URLStateManager {\r\n    constructor(callbacks = {}) {\r\n        this.callbacks = callbacks;\r\n        this.state = this.parseUrlParams();\r\n        \r\n        // Listen for browser back/forward navigation\r\n        window.addEventListener('popstate', (event) =\u003e {\r\n            if (event.state) {\r\n                this.state = event.state;\r\n                this.notifyStateChange();\r\n            }\r\n        });\r\n    }",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "/**\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": " * URL State Manager\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": " * \r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": " * Manages synchronization between application state and URL parameters\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": " * Provides browser history support and shareable links\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": " */\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "class URLStateManager {\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "    constructor(callbacks = {}) {\r"

        "originalContents": "/**\r\n * URL State Manager\r\n * \r\n * Manages synchronization between application state and URL parameters\r\n * Provides browser history support and shareable links\r\n */\r\n\r\nclass URLStateManager {\r\n    constructor(callbacks = {}) {\r\n        this.callbacks = callbacks;\r\n        this.state = this.parseUrlParams();\r\n        \r\n        // Listen for browser back/forward navigation\r\n        window.addEventListener('popstate', (event) =\u003e {\r\n            if (event.state) {\r\n                this.state = event.state;\r\n                this.notifyStateChange();\r\n            }\r\n        });\r\n    }",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 1,
            "line": 152
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "app/static/js/core/url-state-manager.js"
      },
      "score": 0.1870117038488388
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "        async function refreshActiveComponents() {\r\n            const currentPath = window.location.pathname;\r\n            \r\n            try {\r\n                if (currentPath.includes('/league/')) {\r\n                    await refreshLeagueComponents();\r\n                } else if (currentPath.includes('/team/')) {\r\n                    await refreshTeamComponents();\r\n                } else if (currentPath.includes('/player/')) {\r\n                    await refreshPlayerComponents();\r\n                } else {\r\n                    // Generic refresh for other pages\r\n                    await refreshGenericComponents();\r\n                }\r\n            } catch (error) {\r\n                console.error('Error refreshing components:', error);\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        async function refreshLeagueComponents() {\r\n            console.log('🔄 Refreshing league components...');\r\n            \r\n            // Try to refresh league-specific components\r\n            const methods = [\r\n                'updateSeasonButtons',\r\n                'updateLeagueButtons',\r\n                'updateWeekButtons', \r\n                'updateTeamButtons',\r\n                'updateTableLeagueHistory',\r\n                'updateTableLeagueWeek',\r\n                'updateChartsLeague'\r\n            ];\r\n            \r\n            for (const method of methods) {\r\n                if (typeof window[method] === 'function') {\r\n                    try {\r\n                        await window[method]();\r\n                    } catch (error) {\r\n                        console.warn(&#96;Error calling ${method}:&#96;, error);\r\n                    }\r\n                }\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n",

        "originalContents": "from flask import Blueprint, render_template, jsonify, request\r\nfrom app.services.league_service import LeagueService\r\nfrom data_access.schema import Columns, ColumnsExtra\r\nfrom app.services.i18n_service import i18n_service, Language\r\nfrom business_logic.statistics import query_database\r\nfrom data_access.adapters.data_adapter_factory import DataAdapterSelector\r\nimport traceback\r\nfrom app.services.data_dict import DataDict\r\n\r\nbp = Blueprint('league', __name__)\r\n\r\ndef get_league_service():\r\n    \"\"\"Helper function to get LeagueService with database parameter\"\"\"\r\n    database = request.args.get('database')\r\n    print(f\"🔄 get_league_service called with database: {database}\")\r\n    league_service = LeagueService(database=database)\r\n    print(f\"✅ LeagueService created with database: {getattr(league_service, 'database', 'None')}\")\r\n    return league_service",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_league_week_table')\r\ndef get_league_week_table():\r\n    try:\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n        # week should be int\r\n        week = int(request.args.get('week'))\r\n        # print(\"get_league_week\")\r\n        print(f\"🔄 League Standings - Received request with: season={season}, league={league}, week={week}\")\r\n        \r\n        if not season or not league:\r\n            return jsonify({\"error\": i18n_service.get_text(\"season_league_required\")}), 400\r\n\r\n        # Get the table data from the service\r\n        # This now returns a TableData object\r\n        league_service = get_league_service()\r\n        print(f\"✅ LeagueService created with database: {getattr(league_service, 'database', 'None')}\")\r\n        \r\n        table_data = league_service.get_league_week_table_simple(season=season, league=league, week=week)\r\n        print(f\"✅ League week table data: {table_data.to_dict() if table_data else 'None'}\")\r\n        \r\n        #print(\"############################\")\r\n        #print(\"league_routes.get_league_week_table: \", end=\"\")\r\n        #print(table_data)\r\n        \r\n        #print(\"############################\")\r\n        \r\n        if not table_data:\r\n            return jsonify({\"message\": \"No data found for these filters\"}), 404\r\n        \r\n        # Convert TableData to dictionary and return as JSON\r\n        return jsonify(table_data.to_dict())\r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        import traceback\r\n        print(f\"❌ Error in get_league_week: {str(e)}\")\r\n        print(f\"Traceback: {traceback.format_exc()}\")  # This will show the full error trace\r\n        return jsonify({\"error\": str(e)}), 500",
        "detailedLines": [

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_league_week_table')\r\ndef get_league_week_table():\r\n    try:\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n        # week should be int\r\n        week = int(request.args.get('week'))\r\n        # print(\"get_league_week\")\r\n        print(f\"🔄 League Standings - Received request with: season={season}, league={league}, week={week}\")\r\n        \r\n        if not season or not league:\r\n            return jsonify({\"error\": i18n_service.get_text(\"season_league_required\")}), 400\r\n\r\n        # Get the table data from the service\r\n        # This now returns a TableData object\r\n        league_service = get_league_service()\r\n        print(f\"✅ LeagueService created with database: {getattr(league_service, 'database', 'None')}\")\r\n        \r\n        table_data = league_service.get_league_week_table_simple(season=season, league=league, week=week)\r\n        print(f\"✅ League week table data: {table_data.to_dict() if table_data else 'None'}\")\r\n        \r\n        #print(\"############################\")\r\n        #print(\"league_routes.get_league_week_table: \", end=\"\")\r\n        #print(table_data)\r\n        \r\n        #print(\"############################\")\r\n        \r\n        if not table_data:\r\n            return jsonify({\"message\": \"No data found for these filters\"}), 404\r\n        \r\n        # Convert TableData to dictionary and return as JSON\r\n        return jsonify(table_data.to_dict())\r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        import traceback\r\n        print(f\"❌ Error in get_league_week: {str(e)}\")\r\n        print(f\"Traceback: {traceback.format_exc()}\")  # This will show the full error trace\r\n        return jsonify({\"error\": str(e)}), 500",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_league_history')\r\ndef get_league_history():\r\n    try:\r\n        # Get query parameters\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n        \r\n        print(f\"League History - Received request with: season={season}, league={league}\")\r\n        \r\n        if not all([season, league]):\r\n            return jsonify({'error': 'Missing required parameters'}), 400\r\n        \r\n        # Use the new method that returns TableData\r\n        league_service = get_league_service()\r\n        table_data = league_service.get_league_history_table_data(\r\n            league_name=league,\r\n            season=season\r\n        )\r\n        \r\n        #print(\"::::::::::::::::::::::::::::\")\r\n        #print(table_data)\r\n        #print(\"::::::::::::::::::::::::::::\")\r\n        \r\n        # Convert to dictionary and return as JSON\r\n        return jsonify(table_data.to_dict())\r\n        \r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"Error: {str(e)}\")\r\n        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 500",

        ],
        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_league_history')\r\ndef get_league_history():\r\n    try:\r\n        # Get query parameters\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n        \r\n        print(f\"League History - Received request with: season={season}, league={league}\")\r\n        \r\n        if not all([season, league]):\r\n            return jsonify({'error': 'Missing required parameters'}), 400\r\n        \r\n        # Use the new method that returns TableData\r\n        league_service = get_league_service()\r\n        table_data = league_service.get_league_history_table_data(\r\n            league_name=league,\r\n            season=season\r\n        )\r\n        \r\n        #print(\"::::::::::::::::::::::::::::\")\r\n        #print(table_data)\r\n        #print(\"::::::::::::::::::::::::::::\")\r\n        \r\n        # Convert to dictionary and return as JSON\r\n        return jsonify(table_data.to_dict())\r\n        \r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"Error: {str(e)}\")\r\n        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 500",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_individual_averages')\r\ndef get_individual_averages():\r\n    \"\"\"Get individual player averages for a season, optionally filtered by week and/or team\"\"\"\r\n    try:\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        week_str = request.args.get('week')  # Optional parameter\r\n        team = request.args.get('team')  # Optional parameter\r\n        \r\n        if not all([league, season]):\r\n            return jsonify({'error': 'Missing required parameters: league, season'}), 400\r\n        \r\n        # Parse week if provided\r\n        week = None\r\n        if week_str:\r\n            try:\r\n                week = int(week_str)\r\n            except ValueError:\r\n                return jsonify({'error': 'Invalid week parameter: must be an integer'}), 400\r\n        \r\n        filter_info = \"\"\r\n        if week is not None:\r\n            filter_info += f\", week={week}\"\r\n        if team is not None:\r\n            filter_info += f\", team={team}\"\r\n        print(f\"🔄 Individual Averages - Received request with: league={league}, season={season}{filter_info}\")\r\n        \r\n        league_service = get_league_service()\r\n        print(f\"✅ LeagueService created with database: {getattr(league_service, 'database', 'None')}\")\r\n        table_data = league_service.get_individual_averages(league=league, season=season, week=week, team=team)\r\n        print(f\"✅ Individual averages data: {table_data.to_dict() if table_data else 'None'}\")\r\n        return jsonify(table_data.to_dict())\r\n        \r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"❌ Error in get_individual_averages: {str(e)}\")\r\n        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 500",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_individual_averages')\r\ndef get_individual_averages():\r\n    \"\"\"Get individual player averages for a season, optionally filtered by week and/or team\"\"\"\r\n    try:\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n        season = request.args.get('season')\r\n        week_str = request.args.get('week')  # Optional parameter\r\n        team = request.args.get('team')  # Optional parameter\r\n        \r\n        if not all([league, season]):\r\n            return jsonify({'error': 'Missing required parameters: league, season'}), 400\r\n        \r\n        # Parse week if provided\r\n        week = None\r\n        if week_str:\r\n            try:\r\n                week = int(week_str)\r\n            except ValueError:\r\n                return jsonify({'error': 'Invalid week parameter: must be an integer'}), 400\r\n        \r\n        filter_info = \"\"\r\n        if week is not None:\r\n            filter_info += f\", week={week}\"\r\n        if team is not None:\r\n            filter_info += f\", team={team}\"\r\n        print(f\"🔄 Individual Averages - Received request with: league={league}, season={season}{filter_info}\")\r\n        \r\n        league_service = get_league_service()\r\n        print(f\"✅ LeagueService created with database: {getattr(league_service, 'database', 'None')}\")\r\n        table_data = league_service.get_individual_averages(league=league, season=season, week=week, team=team)\r\n        print(f\"✅ Individual averages data: {table_data.to_dict() if table_data else 'None'}\")\r\n        return jsonify(table_data.to_dict())\r\n        \r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"❌ Error in get_individual_averages: {str(e)}\")\r\n        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 500",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/team/\u003canalysis\u003e')\r\ndef team_stats(analysis):\r\n    season = session.get('selected_season', 'all')\r\n    return render_template('team/stats.html', analysis=analysis, season=season)",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n\r\n# ==========================================\r\n# AGGREGATION ENDPOINTS (League-wide over time)\r\n# ==========================================\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_league_averages_history')\r\ndef get_league_averages_history():\r\n    \"\"\"Get league average scores across all seasons\"\"\"\r\n    try:\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n        \r\n        if not league:\r\n            return jsonify({'error': 'Missing required parameter: league'}), 400\r\n        \r\n        print(f\"League Averages History - Received request with: league={league}\")\r\n        \r\n        debug = request.args.get('debug', 'false').lower() == 'true'\r\n        league_service = get_league_service()\r\n        data = league_service.get_league_averages_history(league=league, debug=debug)\r\n        return jsonify(data)\r\n        \r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"Error in get_league_averages_history: {str(e)}\")\r\n        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 500",

          },

            "lineNumber": 528,
            "text": "        \r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 529,
            "text": "        debug = request.args.get('debug', 'false').lower() == 'true'\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 530,
            "text": "        league_service = get_league_service()\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 531,
            "text": "        data = league_service.get_league_averages_history(league=league, debug=debug)\r"

        ],
        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n# ==========================================\r\n# AGGREGATION ENDPOINTS (League-wide over time)\r\n# ==========================================\r\n\r\n@bp.route('/league/get_league_averages_history')\r\ndef get_league_averages_history():\r\n    \"\"\"Get league average scores across all seasons\"\"\"\r\n    try:\r\n        league = request.args.get('league')\r\n        \r\n        if not league:\r\n            return jsonify({'error': 'Missing required parameter: league'}), 400\r\n        \r\n        print(f\"League Averages History - Received request with: league={league}\")\r\n        \r\n        debug = request.args.get('debug', 'false').lower() == 'true'\r\n        league_service = get_league_service()\r\n        data = league_service.get_league_averages_history(league=league, debug=debug)\r\n        return jsonify(data)\r\n        \r\n    except Exception as e:\r\n        print(f\"Error in get_league_averages_history: {str(e)}\")\r\n        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 500",
